description: Manage n8n server - workflows, nodes, executions, credentials, data tables, and logs

auth.usage_instructions: Authentication lifecycle: login to store credentials, check status, test that they work, refresh OAuth tokens, or logout. Runs per-profile when --profile is used.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.
login.usage_instructions: Configure and store n8n credentials interactively. Prompts for API key (or other credential type). Use --profile/-p to store under a named profile; --force/-F overwrites existing credentials without confirmation.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

logout.usage_instructions: Clear stored credentials and any browser session for the current or named profile. Use --force/-F to skip confirmation.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

profiles.usage_instructions: Manage multiple named authentication profiles. Useful for working against multiple n8n instances (dev/staging/prod). The default profile is used when --profile is omitted.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Create a new profile from .env.example template. Prompts for each required field. Follow up with `auth login --profile <name>` to populate credentials.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a profile and all its stored data. Use --force/-F to skip confirmation.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.
get.usage_instructions: Show details for a specific profile by name, including which env vars are set (values masked).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.
list.usage_instructions: List all authentication profiles and show which one is the default. Use --table/-t for a human-readable view.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test``.
status.usage_instructions: Check whether credentials are configured AND valid by making a real API call. Returns JSON with `authenticated: true/false`. Use --table/-t for a human view.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Verify credentials work end-to-end by calling the n8n API. Use when debugging auth issues -- distinguishes 'not configured' from 'configured but invalid'.

cache.usage_instructions: Manage the local response cache that speeds up repeat API queries. Use `cache clear` to force a fresh fetch.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Remove all cached responses from the local cache directory. Use when you want a guaranteed-fresh fetch from the n8n API.

credentials.help: Manage n8n credentials on the server
credentials.usage_instructions: Manage credentials stored on the n8n server -- list, get, create (from JSON), rename, delete, and show the JSON schema for a credential type.

create.help: Create a credential on the n8n server. The credential type must match a type defined by an installed node. Use 'n8n credentials schema <type>' to see required fields. Example: n8n credentials create brickowlApi '{"apiKey": "abc123"}' n8n credentials create brickowlApi '{"apiKey": "abc"}' --name "Brickowl Prod"
create.usage_instructions: Create a credential on the server. The credential type must match one of the schemas returned by `credentials schema`. Pass the credential data as JSON.

delete.help: Delete a credential from the n8n server. Example: n8n credentials delete qe03iDk3T8FNbNTg
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a credential by ID. Workflows using it will fail until the credential is replaced.

get.help: Get details for a specific credential by ID. Example: n8n credentials get qe03iDk3T8FNbNTg n8n credentials get qe03iDk3T8FNbNTg --table
get.usage_instructions: Get metadata for a specific credential by ID. Example: `n8n credentials get cred123`.

list.help: List all credentials on the n8n server. Example: n8n credentials list n8n credentials list --table n8n credentials list --filter "type:eq:brickowlApi" n8n credentials list --properties "id,name"
list.usage_instructions: List all credentials on the n8n server. Supports --filter/-f, --limit/-l, --table/-t, --properties/-p. Credentials values are NOT returned -- only metadata.

rename.help: Rename a credential on the n8n server. Example: n8n credentials rename tTBhVO3JjWS6PhOH "Susan Slack Bot"
rename.usage_instructions: Rename a credential without changing its data or ID.

schema.help: Show the JSON schema for a credential type (required fields and properties). Example: n8n credentials schema brickowlApi n8n credentials schema brickfreedomApi
schema.usage_instructions: Show the JSON schema for a credential type (required/optional fields). Use before `credentials create` to see what fields a type expects.

data-tables.usage_instructions: Manage n8n Data Tables (database-like storage). Create/delete tables, inspect columns, and CRUD on rows (`rows`, `insert`, `update-rows`, `delete-rows`).

columns.help: Show columns for a data table. Example: n8n data-tables columns L13pmzZlnNh5hPw7 n8n data-tables columns L13pmzZlnNh5hPw7 --table
columns.usage_instructions: Show column definitions for a data table. Use before `insert` or `update-rows` to verify the schema.

create.help: Create a new data table. Column types: string, number, boolean, date Example: n8n data-tables create "Orders" --column "name:string" --column "total:number" n8n data-tables create "Flags" --column "key:string" --column "enabled:boolean"
create.usage_instructions: Create a new data table. Column types supported: string, number, boolean, date. Pass a name and column spec.

delete.help: Delete a data table. Example: n8n data-tables delete L13pmzZlnNh5hPw7
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a data table and all its rows. Example: `n8n data-tables delete L13pmzZ`.

delete-rows.help: Delete specific rows from a data table. Example: n8n data-tables delete-rows L13pmzZlnNh5hPw7 --row-id row123 n8n data-tables delete-rows L13pmzZlnNh5hPw7 --row-id row1 --row-id row2
delete-rows.usage_instructions: Delete specific rows from a data table by row ID. Example: `n8n data-tables delete-rows <table_id> <row_id>`.

get.help: Get details for a data table by ID, including its columns. Example: n8n data-tables get L13pmzZlnNh5hPw7 n8n data-tables get L13pmzZlnNh5hPw7 --table
get.usage_instructions: Get details for one data table by ID, including its column definitions. Example: `n8n data-tables get L13pmzZ`.

insert.help: Insert rows into a data table. Accepts a JSON array of objects as an argument or via stdin. Example: n8n data-tables insert L13pmzZlnNh5hPw7 '[{"name":"test","value":42}]' echo '[{"name":"test"}]' | n8n data-tables insert L13pmzZlnNh5hPw7
insert.usage_instructions: Insert one or more rows. Accepts a JSON array of objects keyed by column name.

arguments.name: data
arguments.help: JSON array of row objects (or pipe via stdin)

list.help: List all data tables. Example: n8n data-tables list n8n data-tables list --table n8n data-tables list --filter "name:contains:orders" n8n data-tables list --properties "id,name"
list.usage_instructions: List all data tables with --filter/-f, --limit/-l, --table/-t, --properties/-p. Example: `n8n data-tables list`.

rows.help: List rows in a data table. Example: n8n data-tables rows L13pmzZlnNh5hPw7 n8n data-tables rows L13pmzZlnNh5hPw7 --table --limit 10 n8n data-tables rows L13pmzZlnNh5hPw7 --filter "status:eq:active"
rows.usage_instructions: List rows in a data table. Supports --filter/-f for client-side filtering (e.g., `category:eq:memory`), --limit/-l, --table/-t.

update-rows.help: Update a row in a data table by row ID. Accepts a JSON object with the columns to update. Example: n8n data-tables update-rows TABLE_ID 1 '{"last_status":"ok","last_checked":"2026-02-20T14:00:00Z"}'
update-rows.usage_instructions: Update a specific row by row ID. Accepts a JSON object with the columns to update.

arguments.name: data
arguments.help: JSON object of column:value pairs to update

executions.usage_instructions: Query workflow execution history. `list` uses the API (paginated by timeframe), `get` returns full detail for one execution, `events` queries raw event log files via SSH for deeper inspection.

events.help: Query event log files (EventBus) by timeframe via SSH. Parses n8nEventLog*.log JSON files for audit events, workflow events, and node events.
events.usage_instructions: Query raw event log files (EventBus) via SSH. More detailed than the API -- shows node-level events, error text, and timestamps. Use when `executions get` doesn't have enough detail.

options.name: --from
options.help: Start datetime (YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS). Default: start of today

options.name: --type
options.help: Filter by type: audit, workflow, node

options.name: --name
options.help: Filter by event name (substring match, e.g. 'login', 'failed')

get.help: Get details for a specific execution by ID. Example: n8n executions get 12345 n8n executions get 12345 --table n8n executions get 12345 --include-data
get.usage_instructions: Get full details for one execution by ID, including per-node input/output. Example: `n8n executions get 1618`.

list.help: Query execution history by timeframe via the n8n API. Filters by startedAt client-side (the API has no date filter parameter) and merges currently-running executions into the default list (the API omits them unless status=running is requested).
list.usage_instructions: Query execution history via the n8n API. Paginated and filtered by timeframe. Use --filter/-f, --limit/-l, --table/-t to narrow results. Good for 'what ran today' questions.

options.name: --from
options.help: Start datetime (YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS). Default: start of today

options.name: --status
options.help: Filter by status (error, success, running, canceled, waiting)

nodes.usage_instructions: Manage n8n community node packages. Covers converting CLI tools into nodes (`create`), deploying them to the server (`deploy`), installing third-party community packages (`install`), listing/inspecting nodes (`list`, `get`), testing nodes (`test`), and removing them (`remove`). `cli-tools` lists CLI tools available for conversion.

cli-tools.help: List and inspect available CLI tools for node conversion
cli-tools.usage_instructions: List and inspect CLI tools available for conversion into n8n nodes. Helper subgroup used before `nodes create`.

get.help: Inspect a CLI tool's metadata (command groups, commands, parameters, credentials). Example: n8n nodes cli-tools get brickowl n8n nodes cli-tools get shippo --table
get.usage_instructions: Inspect a CLI tool's metadata (command groups, commands, parameters, credentials). Example: n8n nodes cli-tools get brickowl n8n nodes cli-tools get shippo --table. Required arguments: name. Notable options: --table/-t.

list.help: List available CLI tools that can be converted to n8n nodes. Example: n8n nodes cli-tools list n8n nodes cli-tools list --table n8n nodes cli-tools list --limit 10
list.usage_instructions: List available CLI tools that can be converted to n8n nodes. Example: n8n nodes cli-tools list n8n nodes cli-tools list --table n8n nodes cli-tools list --limit 10. Notable options: --table/-t, --limit/-l, --filter/-f, --properties/-p.

create.help: Convert a CLI tool into an n8n community node package. Parses the CLI tool's commands, parameters, and credentials, then generates a complete n8n node package with TypeScript source, credentials, README, and a bundled copy of the CLI tool source (under cli/). The CLI venv is created on the server during deploy. Example: n8n nodes create brickowl n8n nodes create shippo --output-dir ./output n8n nodes create brickowl --force n8n nodes create slack --name slack-custom --display-name "Slack (Custom)"
create.usage_instructions: Convert a standardized CLI tool into an n8n community node package. Parses the CLI's commands/options and generates the node source in the cli-tools repo.

options.name: --display-name
options.help: Override display name in n8n UI

deploy.help: Deploy an n8n node package to the n8n server (adam-server). Performs the full pipeline: build TypeScript, rsync to server, npm install into community nodes dir (~/.n8n/nodes/), install bundled CLI venv, create n8n credentials from CLI .env, restart n8n, and verify the node loads. Example: n8n nodes deploy shippo n8n nodes deploy ~/Dropbox/GitRepos/n8n-nodes/brickowl n8n nodes deploy ~/Dropbox/GitRepos/n8n-nodes-claudecode n8n nodes deploy ~/Dropbox/GitRepos/n8n-nodes/brickowl --skip-build
deploy.usage_instructions: Deploy a generated/modified n8n node package to the n8n server (adam-server). Builds, uploads, and restarts n8n to pick it up.

arguments.name: package_path
arguments.help: Path to the n8n node package directory

get.help: Get details for a node package or built-in node. Without --type, looks up locally generated packages. With --type, fetches the full node definition from the n8n server. Example: n8n nodes get brickowl n8n nodes get slack --type default n8n nodes get slack --type default --table
get.usage_instructions: Get details for a node package or built-in node. Without --type, inspects a local package; with --type, inspects a node on the server.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Package name, CLI tool name, or built-in node name

install.help: Install a third-party community node package from npm. Installs the package through the running n8n instance so npm runs in the same server/runtime environment that loads community packages. Example: n8n nodes install n8n-nodes-duckduckgo-search n8n nodes install @scope/n8n-nodes-example n8n nodes install n8n-nodes-example --version 1.2.3 n8n nodes install n8n-nodes-mcp --skip-verify
install.usage_instructions: Install a third-party community node package from npm through the running n8n server. Pass scoped packages exactly, for example @chrishdx/n8n-nodes-codex-cli-lm; use --version for a pinned npm version and --skip-verify only when you will verify separately.

options.name: --version
options.help: Install a specific npm package version

options.name: --skip-restart
options.help: Deprecated; n8n REST installs load packages without restart

list.help: List n8n node packages. Without --type, lists locally generated packages (from nodes create). With --type, queries the n8n server for installed nodes. Example: n8n nodes list                    # locally generated packages n8n nodes list --type default     # built-in nodes on server n8n nodes list --type community   # third-party community nodes on server n8n nodes list --type custom      # generated/custom packages on server n8n nodes list --type community --include-tools --table
list.usage_instructions: List n8n node packages. Without --type, lists locally generated node packages. Use --type community for third-party packages and --type custom for Adam-generated packages installed on the server. Supports --filter/-f, --limit/-l, --table/-t.

remove.help: Remove a node package from the n8n server. Third-party community packages are uninstalled through the running n8n instance; custom/generated packages are removed from the community nodes directory and n8n is restarted. Example: n8n nodes remove brickowl n8n nodes remove @scope/n8n-nodes-example n8n nodes remove airtable --keep-credentials n8n nodes remove ebay --skip-restart
remove.usage_instructions: Remove a node package from the n8n server by package name. Pass scoped packages exactly, for example @scope/n8n-nodes-example.

test.help: Test an n8n node, or run static health checks on an existing workflow. Two modes: 1. PACKAGE MODE (default): Provide NODE_NAME to test a node package. Creates a temporary workflow with a Webhook trigger -> node, activates, triggers, polls for completion, and verifies success. Before activation, runs the static health-check engine on the temp workflow — config bugs surface immediately instead of failing at runtime. 2. WORKFLOW MODE (--workflow <id>): Runs static health checks on an EXISTING workflow. No execution. Validates loadOptions, required params, credentials, typeVersion, sub-workflow refs, webhook uniqueness, expression node refs, connectivity, and pinData orphans. Requires: - n8n API credentials in /Users/adam/Dropbox/GitRepos/Agents/skills/n8n/.env - For package mode: the node package must already be installed on the n8n server Example: n8n nodes test claudecode -p '{"prompt":"What is 2+2?","model":"haiku","outputFormat":"text"}' n8n nodes test brickowl --resource order --operation list --timeout 120 n8n nodes test --workflow U7cK5XlQqmgG9CWlrB6wM n8n nodes test --workflow U7cK5XlQqmgG9CWlrB6wM --node "Incoming Slack Message" --strict --json
test.usage_instructions: Test n8n nodes in two modes. PACKAGE MODE (default): pass NODE_NAME to spin up a temporary Webhook -> node workflow that exercises a freshly installed node package end-to-end (activate, trigger via webhook, poll for completion, verify success); before activation, the static health-check engine runs on the temp workflow so config bugs surface immediately. Use during node development to validate behavior. WORKFLOW MODE: pass --workflow <id> to run static health checks against an EXISTING workflow without executing it — validates loadOptions, required params, credentials, typeVersion, sub-workflow refs, webhook uniqueness, expression node refs, connectivity, and pinData orphans. Combine with --node "<name>" to scope checks to a single node, --strict to promote warnings to failures (non-zero exit on any warning), and --json to emit findings as JSON instead of a table.

arguments.name: node_name
arguments.help: Name of the n8n node to test (e.g., claudecode). Optional when --workflow is used

options.name: --params
options.help: Extra node parameters as JSON string

options.name: --credentials
options.help: Node credentials as JSON string, e.g. '{"claudeCodeApi":{"id":"abc","name":"My Cred"}}'

options.name: --username
options.help: Username for browser automation nodes (required when node uses browser_session credential)

options.name: --password
options.help: Password for browser automation nodes (required when node uses browser_session credential)

options.name: --workflow
options.help: Run static health checks on an existing workflow by ID. Skips temp workflow creation/execution

options.name: --node
options.help: In --workflow mode, restrict checks to the node with this exact name

options.name: --strict
options.help: In --workflow mode, promote warn-level findings to fail and exit non-zero on any warning

options.name: --json
options.help: Emit health findings as JSON (instead of a table)

server.usage_instructions: Manage the n8n server process itself -- upgrade to a new version, check version, restart, and inspect logs/config via the `logs` and `config` subgroups.

config.usage_instructions: Subgroup for managing n8n server configuration. Run `n8n server config --help` to see available commands.

set.help: Set an n8n server configuration variable in the LaunchDaemon plist.
set.usage_instructions: Set an n8n server configuration variable in the LaunchDaemon plist. Required arguments: key, value. Notable options: --restart, --plist. Example: `n8n server config set EXECUTIONS_DATA_MAX_AGE 168`.

arguments.name: key
arguments.help: Config key: EXECUTIONS_DATA_PRUNE, EXECUTIONS_DATA_MAX_AGE, EXECUTIONS_DATA_PRUNE_MAX_COUNT, N8N_WORKFLOW_HISTORY_PRUNE_TIME

show.help: Show current n8n server pruning configuration. Example: n8n server config show
show.usage_instructions: Show current n8n server pruning configuration. Example: n8n server config show. Notable options: --plist.

logs.help: Query n8n server logs and configuration
logs.usage_instructions: Subgroup for querying n8n server logs (packages, entries, etc). Run `n8n server logs --help` to see available commands.

all.help: Combined troubleshooting dump: executions + events + app logs + error logs. Collects from all 4 data sources for the given timeframe and outputs a structured JSON report.
all.usage_instructions: Combined troubleshooting dump: executions + events + app logs + error logs. Collects from all 4 data sources for the given timeframe and outputs a structured JSON report. Notable options: --from/-f, --to/-t, --limit/-l, --data-dir. Example: `n8n server logs all --from 2026-02-12`.

options.name: --from
options.help: Start datetime. Default: start of today

app.help: View application logs from the n8n server via SSH.
app.usage_instructions: View application logs from the n8n server via SSH. Notable options: --lines/-n, --log-file. Example: `n8n server logs app`.

config.help: Show current logging configuration from the n8n LaunchDaemon plist. Example: n8n server logs config
config.usage_instructions: Show current logging configuration from the n8n LaunchDaemon plist. Example: n8n server logs config. Notable options: --plist.

errors.help: View error logs from the n8n server via SSH.
errors.usage_instructions: View error logs from the n8n server via SSH. Notable options: --lines/-n, --log-file. Example: `n8n server logs errors`.

options.name: --log-file
options.help: Error log file path on server

set-level.help: Set n8n log level and optionally other logging settings. Modifies the LaunchDaemon plist on the server and restarts n8n.
examples: n8n server logs set-level debug --format json --output console,file
set-level.usage_instructions: Set n8n log level and optionally other logging settings. Modifies the LaunchDaemon plist on the server and restarts n8n. Required arguments: level. Notable options: --format, --output, --db-logging, --restart, --plist. Example: `n8n server logs set-level debug`.

arguments.name: level
arguments.help: Log level: silent, error, warn, info, debug

restart.help: Restart the n8n server. Example: n8n server restart
restart.usage_instructions: Restart the n8n server process. Required after upgrading or installing community nodes.

upgrade.help: Upgrade n8n to the latest version (or a specific version). Installs the new version globally via npm, restarts the n8n LaunchDaemon, and verifies the server is healthy.
upgrade.usage_instructions: Upgrade n8n to the latest version or a specific version. Accepts a version argument. Use --skip-restart to defer the restart.

version.help: Show the n8n server version. Example: n8n server version
version.usage_instructions: Show the n8n server version. Example: `n8n server version`.

workflows.usage_instructions: Manage n8n workflows on the server -- list, get, create, update, delete, activate/deactivate, export, execute, and assign error handlers. Also contains the `node` subgroup for adding/connecting nodes inside a workflow.

activate.help: Activate a workflow. Example: n8n workflows activate abc123
activate.usage_instructions: Activate a workflow so it runs on its triggers. Example: `n8n workflows activate abc123`.

assign-error-handler.help: Assign an error handler workflow to one or all workflows. Sets settings.errorWorkflow so failures trigger the error handler.
assign-error-handler.usage_instructions: Assign an error-handler workflow to one specific workflow or all workflows. The error workflow is invoked when another workflow fails.

options.name: --error-workflow-id
options.help: Error handler workflow ID to assign

create.help: Create a workflow from a JSON file. The JSON file should contain the workflow definition with nodes and connections. Example: n8n workflows create workflow.json n8n workflows create workflow.json --name "My Workflow" n8n workflows create workflow.json --activate n8n workflows create workflow.json --error-workflow none
create.usage_instructions: Create a workflow from a JSON file containing the full workflow definition. Pass the file path as the argument. The command emits the created workflow JSON on stdout. If a follow-up step needs the workflow ID, save stdout to a file, verify the create command exited 0, then parse `.id` from the saved JSON with `jq -r '.id'` or another JSON parser. Do not grep for `id:` labels.

options.name: --error-workflow
options.help: Error handler workflow ID (default: global handler, use 'none' to skip)

deactivate.help: Deactivate a workflow. Example: n8n workflows deactivate abc123
deactivate.usage_instructions: Deactivate a workflow so triggers are paused. Example: `n8n workflows deactivate abc123`.

delete.help: Delete a workflow from the n8n server. Example: n8n workflows delete abc123 n8n workflows delete abc123 --yes
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a workflow permanently by ID. Example: `n8n workflows delete abc123`.

execute.help: Execute a workflow on the n8n server. Triggers a manual execution via the internal REST API. Works with any workflow regardless of trigger type. Prefers Manual Trigger nodes when available, otherwise uses the first trigger node found.
execute.usage_instructions: Trigger a manual execution of a workflow. Returns the execution ID which can be passed to `executions get` for results.

export.help: Export a workflow as JSON. Exports the full workflow definition including nodes, connections, and settings. Example: n8n workflows export abc123 n8n workflows export abc123 -o workflow-backup.json
export.usage_instructions: Export a workflow as JSON to stdout or a file. Useful for backups or version control of workflow definitions.

get.help: Get full details for a specific workflow by ID. Example: n8n workflows get abc123 n8n workflows get abc123 --table
get.usage_instructions: Get full details for one workflow by ID, including all nodes and connections. Example: `n8n workflows get abc123`.

list.help: List all workflows on the n8n server.
list.usage_instructions: List all workflows on the server. Supports --filter/-f for client-side filtering (e.g., `active:eq:true`, `name:contains:sync`), --limit/-l, --table/-t, --properties/-p to select fields.

node.help: Add and connect nodes in workflows
node.usage_instructions: Subgroup for adding and connecting nodes inside an existing workflow. Use `workflows node --help` to see available actions.

add.help: Add a node to an existing workflow. Resolves the node type from the server, auto-positions it, and optionally connects it to existing nodes.
examples: n8n workflows node add WF_ID slack -r message -o post --after "Manual Trigger"
n8n workflows node add WF_ID slack --between "HTTP Request,Set Fields"
n8n workflows node add WF_ID airtable --name "Read Records" --params '{"baseId":"app123"}'
n8n workflows node add WF_ID emailReadImap --credential "Susan IMAP - DreamHost"
add.usage_instructions: Add a node to an existing workflow. Resolves the node type from the server, auto-positions it, and optionally connects it to existing nodes. Required arguments: workflow_id, node_type. Notable options: --resource/-r, --operation/-o, --after, --between, --name/-n, --params, --position, --credential/-c. Example: `n8n workflows node add WF_ID slack -r message -o post --after "Manual Trigger"`.

options.name: --after
options.help: Connect after this node (by display name)

options.name: --name
options.help: Custom display name for the node

options.name: --credential
options.help: Credential name or ID to attach to the node

connect.help: Connect two existing nodes in a workflow. Creates a connection from the source node's output to the target node's input.
examples: n8n workflows node connect WF_ID --from "Slack" --to "Set Fields"
n8n workflows node connect WF_ID --from "My Tool" --to "AI Agent" --type ai_tool
connect.usage_instructions: Connect two existing nodes in a workflow. Creates a connection from the source node's output to the target node's input. Required arguments: workflow_id. Notable options: --from, --to, --output-index, --input-index, --type/-t. Example: `n8n workflows node connect WF_ID --from "Slack" --to "Set Fields"`.

options.name: --type
options.help: Connection type: main, ai_tool, ai_languageModel, ai_memory

update.help: Update an existing node's parameters, name, or credentials. Merges provided parameters into the node's existing parameters (deep merge).
examples: n8n workflows node update WF_ID "Susan" --params '{"options": {"systemMessage": "new prompt"}}'
n8n workflows node update WF_ID "My Node" --name "Renamed Node"
n8n workflows node update WF_ID "My Node" --credential "New API Key"
update.usage_instructions: Update an existing node's parameters, name, or credentials. Merges provided parameters into the node's existing parameters (deep merge). Required arguments: workflow_id, node_name. Notable options: --params/-p, --name/-n, --credential/-c. Example: `n8n workflows node update WF_ID "Susan" --params '{"options": {"systemMessage": "new prompt"}}'`.

arguments.name: node_name
arguments.help: Node display name or node ID

options.name: --params
options.help: Parameters to merge as JSON string

options.name: --name
options.help: New display name for the node

options.name: --credential
options.help: Credential name or ID to attach

update.help: Update an existing workflow. Provide a JSON file to replace the workflow definition, and/or use flags to update name and active status. Example: n8n workflows update abc123 --file updated.json n8n workflows update abc123 --name "New Name" n8n workflows update abc123 --activate n8n workflows update abc123 --deactivate
update.usage_instructions: Update an existing workflow by ID, replacing its definition with the JSON file contents. Use `workflows get` first to fetch the current state.

usage_instructions: Use for managing an n8n automation server -- workflows, nodes, credentials, executions, data tables, server config/logs, and community node packages. Commands follow `n8n <group> <action>` pattern. All list commands support --table/-t, --filter/-f, --limit/-l, and --properties/-p. --filter uses field:op:value syntax (e.g., name:eq:MyWorkflow, active:eq:true). Authentication is managed via `n8n auth login` and stored per-profile.
